name: Spanish Gangster Disciples
description: The Spanish Gangster Disciples (SGD) are a predominantly Indigenous Latino American Folk Nation street gang founded in the early 1980s by Rudy Rios (former Latin Scorpions/MLD member) in South Chicago at 88th & Houston. An offshoot of the Gangster Disciples — Rios transformed the prison-based 'Spanish Growth and Development' concept into a street gang with 'blessings' from the BGD hierarchy. The SGD concept aimed to unify all 'Latin Folks' organizations. A separate Northside chapter formed in 1983 at Clark & Olive, becoming known for high-quality marijuana sales. Founder Rudy Rios was assassinated in 1995, triggering leadership instability — two subsequent leaders were also killed. Internal feuds with 'Demon' (a rogue leader) caused wars with former allies MLDs and Imperial Gangsters. Ambrose took over most of their South Chicago territory by 1997. Still active in Albany Park, suburbs (Elgin, Wheeling, Palatine, Waukegan).
